title: Lastrea platyptera

The classification of Lastrea platyptera is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. The species is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, where it grows in humid environments. The classification of Lastrea platyptera is a reflection of its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Lastrea platyptera, also known as the Broad-leaved Lady Fern, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as respiratory issues and skin infections.
